credit_metrics_b5.notes: All 5 metrics calculable; only Current Ratio passes (1.03x). Debt/EBITDA 8.11x (FAIL), Interest Coverage 1.6x (FAIL), FFO/Debt 7.74% (FAIL), Current Ratio 1.03x (PASS), Debt/Capital 64.3% (FAIL). High leverage from M&A-driven growth. AA credit rating reflects strong parent support and contracted revenue base.
credit_metrics_b5.source: Calculated from JSW Energy FY25 disclosures, Screener.in, Stock Analysis

data_sources.investor_presentation: JSW Energy Q4 FY25 Results Presentation (May 2025)

verification_checklist.coal_capacity_note: 5,658 MW coal + 1,080 MW lignite = 6,738 MW thermal (62%)
verification_checklist.capacity_sums_note: User data shows thermal_mw 5658 but this excludes lignite. Total coal+lignite is 6,738 MW.
verification_checklist.b5_data_note: Complete: OCF 3,838 Cr, Current Assets 12,076 Cr, Current Liabilities 11,715 Cr

analyst_notes: JSW Energy is a diversified IPP with 48% RE share (hydro, solar, wind) and 52% thermal (coal + lignite). Key positives: (1) Ambitious Strategy 3.0 targeting 30 GW RE + 40 GWh storage by 2030; (2) SBTi committed; (3) Net-zero 2050 target; (4) Internal carbon price of $10/tCO2; (5) First mover in green hydrogen with plant commissioned Nov 2025; (6) Strong ESG ratings (MSCI A, S&P Global 77). Key concerns: (1) High leverage (Net D/E 1.6x, Gross Debt/EBITDA 8.1x) from acquisitions; (2) Coal/lignite still 62% of capacity if lignite counted properly; (3) Emissions increased 14.8% YoY. For ETCB: B1 likely FAIL (6,738 MW coal+lignite = 62% at-risk); B2 likely FAIL (CCE ~22% at medium carbon price); B3 likely PASS (no LCOE crossover); B4 possible PASS (net-zero 2050 + high RE trajectory) or FAIL (SBTi not validated, RE <30% currently); B5 FAIL (all calculable metrics fail). Expected score: 1-2/5 Low-medium depending on B4 treatment.
